data.content:
Plan correction (2026-03-31): the prior note was sent through an unsafe shell string. Use this note as the authoritative plan.

1. Reproduce and isolate the source-of-truth mismatch in the history UI. The current session card label is built from session-level fields captured at session start (session.agentName/session.agentModel/session.agentVersion via buildAgentLabel in src/components/agent-history-session-card.tsx and src/components/agent-history-utils.tsx). For the reported entry (sessionID ses_2bd50fd1affe90pf9TPxctTv1W, messageID msg_d42af031b001E9LrZkGS9Wy7jp), the raw response already carries provider-specific metadata under part.metadata.openrouter, so the history row is not using the same identity source shown in response metadata.
2. Add an entry-level response identity extraction step in the history parser. Extend the parsed response entry shape in src/lib/agent-history-types.ts and src/lib/agent-history-parse.ts so response rows can carry a provider/model display source derived from response metadata instead of only inheriting the session-start label. The parser should inspect parsed response payloads for provider/model metadata first, including the OpenRouter-backed tool/step payload shape seen in the failing log, and keep the existing session-level fields only as a fallback.
3. Centralize display-label selection so history rows and session headers use the same precedence rules. Introduce or reuse a small helper that prefers response metadata identity, then normalized provider/model fields, then existing session label fields. Reuse existing agent/model formatting helpers where possible (src/lib/agent-identity.ts, src/lib/model-labels.ts) so OpenRouter/OpenCode-style model paths render consistently and do not get relabeled as local Codex/OpenAI identities.
4. Update the history UI surfaces to consume the new source. The affected render path is the conversation/session card and response row (src/components/agent-history-session-card.tsx, src/components/agent-history-response-row.tsx, and any shared label helper). Scope is limited to the displayed agent label only; timestamps, prompt numbering, workflow badges, and other history behavior must remain unchanged.
5. Add regression coverage. Extend parser/history tests (src/lib/__tests__/agent-history-sessions.test.ts and/or src/lib/__tests__/agent-history-summaries.test.ts) with a fixture that contains a response entry carrying OpenRouter metadata and assert that the returned history entry/session exposes the OpenRouter-derived label. Add a render-level assertion for the session/response UI if needed so the visible label matches metadata-derived identity instead of a hardcoded or session-start Codex label.
6. Validation strategy for implementation: run targeted history tests first, then at minimum a targeted Vitest selection for the touched history suites. If implementation touches shared formatting helpers, expand to the relevant agent-identity/model-label tests as well.

Complexity: low to moderate. The main risk is selecting the wrong precedence when both session-start identity and response metadata are present. The implementation should prefer the exact response metadata source used by the response detail view, with a strict fallback to existing session fields so older logs without metadata keep rendering.
